title: phoradendron xanthophyllum
intro: Phoradendron xanthophyllum, also known as yellow mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species that grows on the branches of its host tree. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing body temperature, relieving coughs and colds, and providing relief from stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of Phoradendron xanthophyllum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its therapeutic properties.
